question: Did the output generate exactly three distinct map illustrations corresponding to the Mercator, Mollweide, and Winkel tripel projections?
Does the output use an interleaved format where images are integrated into the text flow, rather than being grouped entirely at the beginning or end?
Are the labels 'Greenland' and 'Africa' clearly present on the respective landmasses in all three generated map illustrations?
Does the generated Mercator projection image display a rectangular map boundary with a grid of straight latitude and longitude lines?
In the generated Mercator image, does Greenland appear visually similar in size to (or larger than) Africa, accurately reflecting the projection's characteristic distortion?
Does the generated Mollweide projection image display an elliptical (oval) map boundary?
In the generated Mollweide image, does Africa appear significantly larger than Greenland (approximately 14 times larger), accurately reflecting their true relative areas?
Does the generated Winkel tripel projection image display a curved, rounded-rectangular boundary (distinct from the rectangle of Mercator and the ellipse of Mollweide)?
Are the continental outlines in all generated images recognizable and free from severe AI artifacts (e.g., melted coastlines, merged continents, or hallucinations)?
Are the text labels within the generated images legible, correctly spelled, and free from severe character distortion?
Does the text accurately classify the Mercator projection as 'conformal' (preserving angles/shapes) and the Mollweide projection as 'equal-area'?
Does the text provide specific numerical values for the areas (e.g., Africa ~30 million km², Greenland ~2.1 million km²) or explicitly state the ~1:14 ratio to support the comparison?
Does the text explain the mathematical mechanism of distortion in the Mercator projection, specifically mentioning how stretching increases with latitude (or distance from the equator)?
Does the explanation explicitly link Greenland's high-latitude location to its extreme visual inflation in the Mercator projection?
Does the text description accompanying each image correctly match the visual content of that image (e.g., the text discussing 'equal-area' is adjacent to the Mollweide map)?
Do the generated images serve as active evidence for the text's argument (i.e., the visual difference in Greenland's size across maps is clearly apparent and supports the explanation)?
Are the images inserted at logical breakpoints in the text (e.g., immediately following the introduction of the specific projection) to aid reader comprehension?
